title: Install an aquarium light
description: Install LED lighting with a safe photoperiod, verify plant-response evidence, and recover from stress signs before normal operation.

dialogue.text: Your Walstad tank needs stable light, not random blasts. Confirm the fixture, timer, and splash-safe cable routing before we energize anything.

options.text: Bench is dry, timer is ready, and cable has a drip loop.
The cable path is still unsafe or wet.

dialogue.text: Pause before power-on: dry the rim, move the cable below outlet height, and keep all plugs off the floor. Then restart installation.
Mount the LED fixture over open water, avoid hotspot glare, and set an 8-hour baseline photoperiod before fish lights-on.

options.text: Mount the aquarium light and set an 8-hour photoperiod
Fixture is mounted and timer set to 8 hours.

dialogue.text: After one cycle, verify plant and fish response. Proceed only if algae bloom and fish stress are absent.

options.text: Plants are upright, fish behavior is normal, and glare is controlled.
Algae or fish stress signs appeared during the first cycle.
A second cycle still shows stress; pause automation and escalate to manual review.

dialogue.text: Recovery branch: reduce duration to 6 hours, raise fixture height one step, and run another observation cycle before closure.

options.text: Adjusted photoperiod and fixture height; run verification again.

dialogue.text: Escalation path: hold the light at 6 hours, pause intensity increases, and run manual diagnostics (water quality, fixture spread, and livestock behavior) before returning to normal progression.

options.text: Manual review logged with a conservative lighting hold plan.

dialogue.text: Great control. Keep photoperiod logs consistent for a week and avoid sudden intensity jumps that destabilize the Walstad balance.
